title: Disc filling and connected sum
speaker: Kai Zehmisch
abstract: In my talk I will report on recent work with Hansjörg Geiges about a strong connection between the topology of a contact manifold and the existence of contractible periodic Reeb orbits. Namely, if the contact manifold appears as non-trivial contact connected sum and has non-trivial fundamental group or torsion-free homology, then the existence is ensured. This generalizes a result of Helmut Hofer in dimension three.

proof_stages.label: Reeb dynamics and Hofer’s motivating theorem
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines contact forms and Reeb vector fields, states the Weinstein conjecture and known cases, and singles out Hofer’s 1993 three-dimensional result whose proof uses holomorphic disk filling and SFT compactness.

proof_stages.label: Connected-sum reformulation and contact index-one surgery
proof_stages.summary: The three-dimensional condition pi_2(M)≠0 is recast through the sphere theorem as a connected-sum phenomenon, and then topological index-one surgery is upgraded to contact surgery using a standard Liouville handle model and its belt sphere.

proof_stages.label: Higher-dimensional connected-sum theorem
proof_stages.summary: The Geiges-Zehmisch theorem is stated: in dimension at least five, if a contact manifold obtained by index-one contact surgery admits a defining form with no contractible closed Reeb orbits, then the surgery must be a connected sum, and under additional topological hypotheses one summand is a sphere or homotopy sphere.

proof_stages.label: Symplectic cobordism built from the handle
proof_stages.summary: For the proof, a symplectic manifold W is assembled from the negative symplectization, an interpolating cobordism between contact forms, and the shell between two handle hypersurfaces, with a Liouville flow that will later project holomorphic data back to the contact manifold.

proof_stages.label: Disk-filling moduli space and compactness
proof_stages.summary: Inside the handle model the speaker defines Lagrangian cylinders and standard holomorphic disks, sets up a parametrized disk moduli space with marked-point constraints, and uses the no-contractible-orbit assumption together with compactness and an energy-monotonicity argument to control its boundary.

proof_stages.label: Evaluation map with degree and cell constraints
proof_stages.summary: The compact moduli space is evaluated, flowed to the boundary, and projected to produce a continuous map of pairs to (M, belt sphere) whose boundary restriction has degree one and whose distinguished marked-point slice lands in a contractible cell of the belt sphere.

proof_stages.label: Topological degree splitting and h-cobordism conclusion
proof_stages.summary: The preimage of the belt sphere splits the moduli-space cycle into two relative maps with degrees differing by one, forcing nonzero degree on one side; lifting and contracting topology through the moduli space then yields separation and, via the h-cobordism theorem, the spherical-summand conclusion.
